codeReview.summary: The refactor removes shared-schema.ts and alignment runtime tests, but it does not actually derive tool schemas from runtime schemas and the new compile-time checks are vacuous, so core feature requirements are not fully satisfied.

issues.description: Tool argument schemas remain manually duplicated instead of being derived from runtime schemas via .omit/.pick/.extend as required. For example, FlowPlanApplyArgsShape and worker/reviewer shapes are handwritten (lines 38-183), and runtime schema exports are only imported as types (lines 4-9), leaving schema drift risk unresolved.

The new type-level checks are tautological and do not validate tool/runtime equivalence: each assertion compares z.input<typeof XSchema> against a type alias that is defined from the same schema (see src/runtime/schema.ts lines 280-286). This cannot catch divergence after deleting tests/schema-alignment.test.ts.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The core-worker procedure does not explicitly guard against vacuous compile-time assertions, allowing a 'followedProcedure: true' handoff even when schema-equivalence checks cannot detect drift.
Shared conventions do not call out that bare z.custom<T>() performs no runtime validation, which enabled permissive tool-boundary schemas during this refactor.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/tools/schemas.ts:82-83,97,102,108,119-121,133,141,152,169; reproduction command returned true for invalid value: bun -e 'import { tool } from "@opencode-ai/plugin"; const z = tool.schema; console.log(z.object({status:z.custom<"ok"|"needs_input">()}).safeParse({status:"bogus"}).success);'

summary: Review failed: while file consolidation happened, the implementation misses the required runtime-schema-derived tool schema construction and the replacement compile-time checks are not meaningful alignment checks.
